Canonical link best practices — learning when to use rel=canonical, noindex, or meta robots directives
Google Search Console — verifying and submitting sitemaps with canonical links
XML Sitemap protocol — understanding the importance of including canonical URLs in XML sitemaps

knowledge.what_it_is: Content aging and timelines refer to the process of tracking, analyzing, and optimizing content performance over time. This involves monitoring metrics such as engagement, views, clicks, and shares to determine which content is most effective and when it should be updated or replaced.
what_you_will_learn: Google Analytics — setting up and using Google Analytics to track website traffic and content performance
Content calendar — creating a schedule for publishing and updating content to ensure consistency and relevance
Timeline analysis — identifying trends and patterns in content performance over time to inform future content decisions
Heatmap tools — using tools like Crazy Egg or Hotjar to analyze user behavior on the website and optimize content placement
Content refresh rates — determining how often to update existing content to keep it fresh and relevant
Content rotation strategies — implementing a plan for rotating out old content and replacing it with new, high-performing content
Content performance metrics — understanding key metrics such as engagement rate, click-through rate, and conversion rate to measure content effectiveness
A/B testing — using tools like VWO or Optimizely to test different versions of content and determine which performs best

knowledge.what_it_is: Conversion tracking is the process of measuring and analyzing user interactions with content, such as clicks, downloads, or form submissions, to evaluate its effectiveness in driving desired outcomes. As a technical writer, you will use conversion tracking tools to measure the impact of your content on business goals.
what_you_will_learn: Google Analytics — setting up event tracking for content interactions (e.g., clicks, scrolls), creating custom dimensions and metrics
Goal setup in Google Analytics — defining desired outcomes (e.g., lead generation, sales) and tracking progress towards them
Event tracking in Adobe Launch — using rules and conditions to trigger events based on user behavior
Conversion rate calculation — understanding the formula for calculating conversion rates from raw data
A/B testing with VWO or Optimizely — designing experiments to measure the impact of content changes on conversion rates
Heatmap analysis with Crazy Egg or Hotjar — visualizing user interactions to identify areas for improvement
Segmentation in Google Analytics — dividing users into groups based on behavior, demographics, or firmographic data
Attribution modeling in Google Tag Manager — assigning credit to specific touchpoints for conversions

knowledge.what_it_is: Link shorteners and tracking tools are used by technical writers to monitor engagement with content, such as clicks, shares, and downloads. These tools provide valuable insights into how readers interact with written content, helping writers refine their strategies for maximum impact.
what_you_will_learn: Bitly API — using the official Bitly API to track link clicks, shares, and other engagement metrics
Google Analytics UTM parameters — setting up UTM tracking in Google Analytics to monitor campaign performance
ClickMagick — using ClickMagick's advanced features for affiliate link tracking, split testing, and click rotators
Rebrandly API — leveraging the Rebrandly API to create custom branded links with analytics and tracking capabilities
LinkTrackr — utilizing LinkTrackr's real-time analytics and reporting features for link performance monitoring

knowledge.what_it_is: Platform tracking and metrics refer to the process of collecting, analyzing, and visualizing data on user engagement, content performance, and other key metrics to inform technical writing decisions. This involves using various tools and platforms to track website analytics, social media metrics, and content consumption patterns.
what_you_will_learn: Google Analytics — setting up and tracking website traffic, behavior, and conversion events
Matomo (Piwik) — self-hosted web analytics platform for custom data collection and analysis
Hotjar — heatmaps, session recording, and user feedback tools for improving content usability
Content performance metrics — understanding engagement rates, click-through rates, and time on page
SEO tracking — monitoring keyword rankings, backlinks, and crawl errors with tools like Ahrefs or SEMrush
Social media analytics — using Hootsuite Insights or Sprout Social to track social media metrics and engagement
Content consumption patterns — analyzing user behavior data from platforms like Medium or WordPress
Custom event tracking — setting up custom events in Google Analytics for specific content interactions

knowledge.what_it_is: Content distribution channels are platforms, tools, and strategies used by technical writers to share content with target audiences, increasing visibility, engagement, and reach.
what_you_will_learn: Web scraping — extracting relevant information from websites using tools like Beautiful Soup or Scrapy for research and content creation
API documentation portals — creating and publishing API reference materials on platforms like SwaggerHub, ReDoc, or Apiary
Content management systems (CMS) — understanding the role of CMS in managing and distributing content to various channels, including WordPress, Drupal, or Joomla
Single-source authoring tools — using tools like MadCap Flare, Adobe FrameMaker, or RoboHelp to create and publish content across multiple formats and channels
Content delivery networks (CDNs) — optimizing content distribution with CDNs like Cloudflare, MaxCDN, or KeyCDN for faster page loads and reduced latency
Social media platforms — leveraging social media channels like LinkedIn, Twitter, or Facebook to share technical content and engage with target audiences
Blogging platforms — understanding the role of blogging platforms like Medium, Ghost, or WordPress in sharing technical content and building personal brands
Email newsletters — creating and publishing email newsletters using tools like Mailchimp, ConvertKit, or Substack for targeted content distribution

knowledge.what_it_is: Content distribution amplification strategies involve leveraging various channels and techniques to increase the visibility, reach, and engagement of technical content, making it more accessible to target audiences.
what_you_will_learn: Influencer outreach — partnering with industry influencers to promote technical content through their social media channels
Content syndication — republishing or re-distributing high-quality technical content on other reputable websites and platforms
SEO optimization for technical content — applying search engine optimization techniques to improve the discoverability of technical articles, blog posts, and guides
Social media content promotion — using social media advertising and organic reach to amplify technical content's visibility among target audiences
Content partnerships — collaborating with other organizations or companies to co-create, promote, and distribute high-quality technical content
Email newsletter distribution — sharing technical content through targeted email newsletters to subscribers and relevant professionals
Web scraping for content discovery — using web scraping techniques to identify and extract relevant technical information from online sources
Content recommendation engines — implementing algorithms that suggest relevant technical content based on user behavior, preferences, and interests

knowledge.what_it_is: OpenGraph data refers to metadata associated with online content, used by social media platforms and search engines to provide rich previews of web pages. As a Technical Writer, you will work with OpenGraph data to create engaging and informative content that drives user engagement.
what_you_will_learn: Understanding OpenGraph protocol — the standard for sharing metadata between websites and social media platforms
OpenGraph tags — HTML attributes used to specify metadata such as title, description, image URLs, and more
Facebook's OpenGraph API — a set of APIs for retrieving and updating OpenGraph data on Facebook
Twitter Card markup — a way to add metadata to tweets using OpenGraph tags
Schema.org integration — incorporating OpenGraph data with schema.org vocabulary for enhanced SEO
OpenGraph image optimization — best practices for resizing, compressing, and serving images for social media previews
Content preview generation — using OpenGraph data to create dynamic content previews on social media platforms
